articles.title: Kalshi Says It Channels the Wisdom of Crowds. It Just Needs More Women.
articles.summary: Kalshi is facing a gender imbalance problem, with women apparently underrepresented on its prediction markets platform. Diverse crowds make better predictions, so if Kalshi can't fix its demographics, it risks becoming just another echo chamber of overconfident dudes betting on the same biases.

articles.title: The Bettors on Polymarket Don't Think Bitcoin Will Hit $150,000 in March. Here's What I Think.
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are betting against Bitcoin reaching $150,000 by March, suggesting most view the crypto's recent rally as overextended. The market's skepticism creates a clear contrarian setup where either the crowd gets validated or someone makes serious money fading the consensus.

articles.title: The Multibillion-dollar shift turning prediction markets into a professional hedging tool
articles.summary: Institutional money is flooding into prediction markets, transforming them from retail betting venues into sophisticated financial instruments for managing political and economic risk. This professionalization brings deeper liquidity and tighter spreads, but it also means amateur traders face increasingly sharp competition from well-capitalized hedge funds and proprietary trading firms.

articles.title: Insider Trading Is Going to Get People Killed
articles.summary: Stock traders are increasingly using non-public information from defense contractors and government sources to bet on geopolitical events, turning classified intel into market alpha. When prediction markets start pricing in military strikes or diplomatic moves before they happen, governments will crack down hard on both the traders and the platforms that enable them.

articles.title: Exclusive | Kalshi and Polymarket Are Each Eyeing Roughly $20 Billion Valuations
articles.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket are both reportedly seeking valuations around $20 billion in upcoming funding rounds. If accurate, these numbers would cement prediction markets as a legitimate financial sector worth serious money, not just a niche betting curiosity.

articles.title: Free Press Opinion: Prediction markets shouldn’t be so easy to play
articles.summary: A Free Press op-ed argues that prediction markets have become too accessible and user-friendly for casual participants. The criticism reflects growing mainstream attention on prediction markets, which could signal either regulatory scrutiny ahead or simply the usual pearl-clutching that happens when betting goes digital and draws new crowds.

articles.title: MrBeast Fires Employee Accused of Insider Trading With Kalshi Bets
articles.summary: MrBeast fired an employee who allegedly used inside information to place winning bets on Kalshi, likely wagering on outcomes the employee knew in advance through their work at the YouTube empire. The incident highlights how prediction markets are becoming serious enough that even entertainment companies now worry about employees gaming the system with privileged information.

articles.title: Prediction markets are a national security threat
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets pose risks to America's safety and stability, probably because they could leak classified info or manipulate public opinion during crises. If this narrative gains traction with regulators, we could see new restrictions that make it harder to bet on geopolitical events or military outcomes.

articles.title: The prediction market backlash has arrived
articles.summary: Critics are finally pushing back hard against prediction markets after years of hype and mainstream adoption. The timing couldn't be worse for platforms trying to go legit, as regulatory scrutiny and public skepticism could kill the momentum that's been building since 2020.

articles.title: ‘Is This Insider Information?’ The Prediction Market Bets Driving a Campus Frenzy
articles.summary: College students are placing bets on prediction markets using information that might cross the line into insider territory, sparking debates about market integrity on campuses nationwide. If academic insiders are systematically exploiting non-public information about university decisions or research outcomes, it could force platforms to implement stricter verification rules that change how these markets operate.

articles.title: A Technology for a Low-Trust Society
articles.summary: Prediction markets are emerging as the go-to solution for extracting truth from populations that no longer trust traditional institutions or each other. When nobody believes the polls, the pundits, or the official statistics, betting markets become the most reliable signal of what's actually happening in the world.

articles.title: Michigan sues Kalshi, alleges app is skirting state's gambling laws
articles.summary: Michigan's attorney general filed a lawsuit against prediction market Kalshi, claiming the platform violates state gambling regulations by allowing residents to bet on political and other events. The case could set a precedent for how states regulate prediction markets, potentially forcing platforms to block users from jurisdictions that classify their products as gambling rather than financial instruments.
